title: aquilaria khasiana
intro: Aquilaria khasiana, also known as the Khasian agarwood tree, is a species of evergreen tree that belongs to the Thymelaeaceae family. This plant is prized for its fragrant resinous heartwood, which is formed in response to certain types of fungal infections, and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The resinous heartwood of Aquilaria khasiana contains compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The resin can be extracted and used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, extracts, and teas, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and stress relief.
